chunks:
# ==============================================================================
# IVD Recipe: Structured Logging Pattern
# ==============================================================================
# Version: 1.0
# Created: 2026-02-08
# Category: infrastructure_patterns
# ==============================================================================

recipe:
  name: "Structured Logging Pattern"
  version: "1.0"
  category: "infrastructure_patterns"
  created: "2026-02-08"
  author: "Leo Celis"

  description: |
    Standard pattern for adding structured, JSON-line logging to any service
    or tool dispatch layer. Replaces ad-hoc print() calls with structured log
    events that can be parsed, filtered, aggregated, and forwarded by any log
    platform (DigitalOcean, CloudWatch, Datadog, ELK, etc.).

    Key characteristics:
    - Structured JSON lines on stdout (Twelve-Factor App compliant)
    - One canonical log line per operation (Stripe pattern)
    - Environment-aware formatting (pretty for dev, JSON for prod)
    - Fire-and-forget (logging never blocks or crashes the operation)
    - Key dimensions: who, what, when, how long, outcome
    - Privacy-aware (hash identifiers, never log secrets)

  applies_to:
    - "API servers logging request/response cycles"
    - "Tool dispatch layers logging tool calls and results"
    - "Background jobs logging execution and outcomes"
    - "MCP servers tracking tool usage and errors"
    - "CLI tools logging operations for debugging"

  when_to_use:
    - "Service has ad-hoc print() or colored() logging that needs structure"
    - "Need to answer 'who is using what, how often, and is it working?'"
    - "Deploying to a platform that captures stdout (DO, K8s, Lambda, etc.)"
    - "Need usage analytics without adding an external analytics service"
    - "Debugging production issues requires structured, searchable logs"

  when_NOT_to_use:
    - "Need real-time dashboards and funnels (use PostHog/Mixpanel instead)"
    - "Need long-term retention beyond platform log window (add a log sink)"
    - "Compliance requires immutable audit logs (use append-only audit store)"
    - "Logging adds unacceptable latency (sub-microsecond hot paths)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# TEMPLATE SECTIONS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

intent_template:
  summary: "Structured logging for [service name] — [what to track]"
  goal: "Replace ad-hoc logging with structured JSON events so [stakeholder] can answer [key questions] using platform log tools"
  success_metric: "Every [operation] emits exactly one structured log line with [required dimensions]"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Service owner / operator]"
    - "[Alpha users / developers consuming the service]"

constraints_template:
  - name: "one_line_per_operation"
    requirement: "Each [operation] produces exactly one structured log line on completion"
    typical_values: "1 log line per request/tool-call/job — not per step"
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_one_line_per_operation"
    consequence_if_violated: "Log noise; hard to count operations; aggregation brea

older] can answer [key questions] using platform log tools"
  success_metric: "Every [operation] emits exactly one structured log line with [required dimensions]"
  stakeholders:
    - "[Service owner / operator]"
    - "[Alpha users / developers consuming the service]"

constraints_template:
  - name: "one_line_per_operation"
    requirement: "Each [operation] produces exactly one structured log line on completion"
    typical_values: "1 log line per request/tool-call/job — not per step"
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_one_line_per_operation"
    consequence_if_violated: "Log noise; hard to count operations; aggregation breaks"

  - name: "structured_json_in_prod"
    requirement: "Log lines are valid JSON when ENVIRONMENT != 'development'"
    typical_values: "json.dumps() with no newlines in values"
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_json_output_format"
    consequence_if_violated: "Platform log parsers can't index fields; grep is the only tool"

  - name: "required_dimensions"
    requirement: "Every log line includes: event, ts, duration_ms, status"
    typical_values: |
      event: string (operation name, e.g. 'tool_call')
      ts: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp
      duration_ms: int (wall-clock milliseconds)
      status: 'ok' | 'error'
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_required_dimensions"
    consequence_if_violated: "Can't filter, aggregate, or alert on missing fields"

  - name: "no_secrets_logged"
    requirement: "Never log API keys, tokens, passwords, or PII in plaintext"
    typical_values: "Hash identifiers (SHA-256 prefix), redact secrets"
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_no_secrets"
    consequence_if_violated: "Security breach; credential leakage via log platform"

  - name: "fire_and_forget"
    requirement: "Logging failures never crash or slow the operation"
    typical_values: "try/except around log emission; never raise from logger"
    test: "tests/test_logging.py::test_logging_failure_is_silent"
    consequence_if_violated: "Logging bug takes down production service"

rationale_template:
  decision: "Structured JSON logging to stdout for [service]"
  reasoning: |
    - Ad-hoc print()/colored() output is not parseable by log platforms
    - Structured JSON enables filtering by any dimension (tool, user, status)
    - Stdout logging is Twelve-Factor App compliant and works on any platform
    - One canonical log line per operation (Stripe pattern) reduces noise
    - No external dependency needed — just json.dumps() to stdout
    - Privacy: hash API keys, never log secrets or PII in plaintext
  evidence: |
    - structlog best practices: https://www.structlog.org/en/latest/logging-best-practices.html
    - Stripe canonical log lines: widely adopted in production services
    - Twelve-Factor App (Factor XI: Logs): https://12factor.net/logs
    - OpenTelemetry MCP semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/mcp/
  stakeholder: "[Service maintainer]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "keep_print_statements"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Unstructured; can't filter, aggregate, or alert on specific dimensions"
    when_it_works: "Local-only development scripts that never deploy

    - structlog best practices: https://www.structlog.org/en/latest/logging-best-practices.html
    - Stripe canonical log lines: widely adopted in production services
    - Twelve-Factor App (Factor XI: Logs): https://12factor.net/logs
    - OpenTelemetry MCP semantic conventions: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/mcp/
  stakeholder: "[Service maintainer]"

alternatives_template:
  - name: "keep_print_statements"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Unstructured; can't filter, aggregate, or alert on specific dimensions"
    when_it_works: "Local-only development scripts that never deploy to production"

  - name: "external_analytics_service"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Adds external dependency, cost, and data-leaves-infra concerns for alpha stage"
    when_it_works: "Need dashboards, funnels, retention curves (PostHog, Mixpanel); add when 3+ users"

  - name: "database_backed_analytics"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Overkill for alpha; adds managed DB cost and schema maintenance"
    when_it_works: "Need long-term queryable analytics, custom admin dashboards, compliance audit"

  - name: "structlog_library"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Adds dependency for what is 20 lines of json.dumps() code"
    when_it_works: "Large codebase with many logging callsites; need processors, filters, context binding"

  - name: "opentelemetry_tracing"
    typical_rejection_reason: "Heavy infrastructure (collector, backend); overkill for single-service alpha"
    when_it_works: "Multi-service distributed system; need trace propagation across services"

risks_template:
  - condition: "Log volume exceeds platform retention or storage"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Reduce verbosity (remove debug-level events) or add log sink"
    monitor: "Platform log storage usage"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Secrets accidentally logged in plaintext"
    impact: "high"
    action: "Audit log output; add test that greps logs for key patterns (Bearer, sk-, etc.)"
    monitor: "Security review of log samples"
    severity: "high"

  - condition: "JSON serialization fails on exotic types"
    impact: "low"
    action: "Use default=str in json.dumps(); fire-and-forget wrapping"
    monitor: "stderr for serialization tracebacks"
    severity: "low"

  - condition: "Logging overhead affects latency-sensitive paths"
    impact: "medium"
    action: "Profile; move to async logging or batch emission if needed"
    monitor: "p99 latency before/after logging added"
    severity: "low"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    [service]/
    ├── logger.py                     # Structured logger module
    ├── logger_intent.yaml            # Intent artifact (uses this recipe)
    ├── [dispatch_layer].py           # Where log calls are added
    └── tests/
        └── test_logging.py           # Logging constraint tests

  code_structure: |
    # logger.py — Pseudo-code / structure pattern

    # 1. Environment detection
    #    Read

-----------------------------------------------------------------
# IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

implementation_pattern:
  directory_structure: |
    [service]/
    ├── logger.py                     # Structured logger module
    ├── logger_intent.yaml            # Intent artifact (uses this recipe)
    ├── [dispatch_layer].py           # Where log calls are added
    └── tests/
        └── test_logging.py           # Logging constraint tests

  code_structure: |
    # logger.py — Pseudo-code / structure pattern

    # 1. Environment detection
    #    Read ENVIRONMENT env var → "development" or "production"

    # 2. Identity hashing
    #    _hash_key(key) → SHA-256 prefix (8 chars) for privacy

    # 3. Core function: log_event(event, *, duration_ms, status, error, **extra)
    #    - Build entry dict with required fields (event, ts, duration_ms, status)
    #    - Merge optional fields (error, **extra)
    #    - If dev mode → pretty-print one human-readable line
    #    - If prod mode → json.dumps() one JSON line to stdout (flush=True)
    #    - Wrap in try/except → fire-and-forget (never crash from logging)

    # 4. Integration: call log_event() at dispatch layer completion
    #    - On success: log_event("[op]", tool=name, status="ok", duration_ms=elapsed)
    #    - On error:   log_event("[op]", tool=name, status="error", error=str(e))

    # Dependencies: zero (stdlib only — json, hashlib, datetime)

  key_files:
    - file: "logger.py"
      purpose: "Structured log event emitter — JSON for prod, pretty for dev"
      key_functions:
        - name: "log_event"
          purpose: "Emit one canonical structured log line per operation"
        - name: "_hash_key"
          purpose: "Hash identifiers for privacy (API keys, user IDs)"

  dependencies: []  # Zero external dependencies — stdlib only (json, hashlib, datetime)

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# INTEGRATION POINTS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

integration_points:
  - name: "dispatch_layer"
    description: |
      The single place where all operations are routed. Add log_event() here
      for complete coverage with one callsite. Examples:
      - Tool registry call_tool() function
      - API request handler / middleware
      - Job executor run() method
    pattern: |
      Wrap the dispatch function:
      1. Record start time before execution
      2. Execute the operation
      3. On success → log_event with tool name, duration, status="ok"
      4. On error → log_event with tool name, duration, status="error", error message
      5. Always include key_hash for identity tracking

  - name: "auth_layer"
    description: |
      Log authentication attempts — one line per check.
      Three outcomes: success (key_hash), failure (error reason), disabled (note).

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOG LINE SCHEMA
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

log_line_schema:
  required_fields:
    - field: "event"

      3. On success → log_event with tool name, duration, status="ok"
      4. On error → log_event with tool name, duration, status="error", error message
      5. Always include key_hash for identity tracking

  - name: "auth_layer"
    description: |
      Log authentication attempts — one line per check.
      Three outcomes: success (key_hash), failure (error reason), disabled (note).

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# LOG LINE SCHEMA
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

log_line_schema:
  required_fields:
    - field: "event"
      type: "string"
      description: "Operation type (tool_call, auth, health_check)"
      examples: ["tool_call", "auth", "error"]

    - field: "ts"
      type: "string (ISO 8601 UTC)"
      description: "Timestamp of the event"
      examples: ["2026-02-08T14:30:00.123456+00:00"]

    - field: "duration_ms"
      type: "integer"
      description: "Wall-clock time of the operation in milliseconds"
      examples: [12, 670, 3200]

    - field: "status"
      type: "string"
      description: "Outcome of the operation"
      enum: ["ok", "error"]

  optional_fields:
    - field: "tool"
      type: "string"
      description: "Tool or endpoint name"
      examples: ["ivd_search", "ivd_validate"]

    - field: "key_hash"
      type: "string"
      description: "SHA-256 prefix (8 chars) of the API key used"
      examples: ["a3f2c1d4", "none"]

    - field: "error"
      type: "string"
      description: "Error message (truncated to 500 chars)"
      examples: ["Division by zero", "File not found: recipes/bad.yaml"]

    - field: "result_size"
      type: "integer"
      description: "Response size in bytes (optional, for bandwidth tracking)"
      examples: [1024, 45000]

  example_lines:
    - '{"event":"tool_call","ts":"2026-02-08T14:30:00.123Z","duration_ms":670,"status":"ok","tool":"ivd_search","key_hash":"a3f2c1d4"}'
    - '{"event":"tool_call","ts":"2026-02-08T14:30:01.000Z","duration_ms":12,"status":"ok","tool":"ivd_get_context","key_hash":"a3f2c1d4"}'
    - '{"event":"tool_call","ts":"2026-02-08T14:30:02.500Z","duration_ms":50,"status":"error","tool":"ivd_load_recipe","error":"Recipe not found: bad-name","key_hash":"b7e9f0a2"}'
    - '{"event":"auth","ts":"2026-02-08T14:30:03.000Z","duration_ms":1,"status":"error","error":"invalid_key"}'

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# QUERYING LOGS
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

querying_patterns:
  - name: "Count tool calls per tool"
    platform: "doctl / jq"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        jq -r 'select(.event=="tool_call") | .tool' | \
        sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

  - name: "Find errors"
    platform: "doctl / grep"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        grep '"status":"error"'

  - name: "Unique users (by key hash)"
    platform: "doctl / jq"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        jq -r 'select(.event=="tool_call") | .key_hash' | \
        sort -u

  - name: "Slow

------------------------------------

querying_patterns:
  - name: "Count tool calls per tool"
    platform: "doctl / jq"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        jq -r 'select(.event=="tool_call") | .tool' | \
        sort | uniq -c | sort -rn

  - name: "Find errors"
    platform: "doctl / grep"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        grep '"status":"error"'

  - name: "Unique users (by key hash)"
    platform: "doctl / jq"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        jq -r 'select(.event=="tool_call") | .key_hash' | \
        sort -u

  - name: "Slowest operations"
    platform: "doctl / jq"
    command: |
      doctl apps logs <app-id> --type run | \
        jq -r 'select(.event=="tool_call") | "\(.duration_ms)ms \(.tool)"' | \
        sort -rn | head -20

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# VERIFICATION PATTERN
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

verification_pattern:
  continuous_checks:
    - check: "Every operation emits exactly one log line"
      frequency: "per_deploy"
      command: "Run test suite, count log lines per tool call"
      threshold: "1 line per call_tool() invocation"

    - check: "Log lines are valid JSON in prod mode"
      frequency: "per_deploy"
      command: "Run with ENVIRONMENT=production, parse each line with json.loads()"
      threshold: "0 parse failures"

    - check: "No secrets in log output"
      frequency: "per_deploy"
      command: "grep -i 'bearer\\|sk-\\|password\\|secret' in captured logs"
      threshold: "0 matches"

  monitoring_setup:
    - metric: "tool_call count per tool"
      dashboard: "DO App Platform logs / jq"
      alert_threshold: "N/A (informational during alpha)"

    - metric: "error rate"
      dashboard: "DO App Platform logs / grep"
      alert_threshold: "> 10% error rate over 1 hour"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# EXAMPLES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

examples:
  - name: "IVD MCP Server Tool Tracking"
    location: "mcp_server/logger.py"
    domain: "Developer tools / MCP"
    customization: |
      - Added to registry.py call_tool() dispatch layer
      - Tracks tool name, API key hash, duration, and errors
      - JSON output captured by DigitalOcean App Platform logs
      - Pretty output for local development (ENVIRONMENT=development)
    outcome: "Every tool call trackable via doctl apps logs + jq"
    link_to_intent: "mcp_server/logger_intent.yaml"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEST PRACTICES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

best_practices:
  - practice: "One canonical log line per operation"
    why: "Multiple log lines per request create noise and make counting hard (Stripe pattern)"
    example: "Log once at the end of call_tool(), not at start and end"

  - practice: "Log to stdout, let the platform route"
    why: "Twelve-Factor App compliance; works on any platform without config"

trackable via doctl apps logs + jq"
    link_to_intent: "mcp_server/logger_intent.yaml"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# BEST PRACTICES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

best_practices:
  - practice: "One canonical log line per operation"
    why: "Multiple log lines per request create noise and make counting hard (Stripe pattern)"
    example: "Log once at the end of call_tool(), not at start and end"

  - practice: "Log to stdout, let the platform route"
    why: "Twelve-Factor App compliance; works on any platform without config"
    example: "print(json.dumps(entry), flush=True) — no file handlers, no syslog"

  - practice: "Hash identifiers, never log secrets"
    why: "API keys and tokens in logs are a security breach waiting to happen"
    example: "key_hash=sha256(api_key)[:8] instead of api_key=sk-abc..."

  - practice: "Use default=str in json.dumps()"
    why: "Prevents serialization crashes on datetime, Decimal, UUID, etc."
    example: 'json.dumps(entry, default=str)'

  - practice: "Truncate large values"
    why: "Prevents log lines from becoming multi-KB; keeps log storage manageable"
    example: "error=str(e)[:500] — cap error messages at 500 chars"

  - practice: "Environment-aware formatting"
    why: "JSON is unreadable in terminal; pretty output for dev, JSON for prod"
    example: "Check ENVIRONMENT env var; pretty-print in development, JSON otherwise"

common_pitfalls:
  - pitfall: "Logging at every step instead of once per operation"
    symptom: "10+ log lines per request; hard to count operations; log volume explodes"
    fix: "Log once at dispatch layer completion — include all dimensions in that one line"

  - pitfall: "Logging secrets in plaintext"
    symptom: "API keys visible in DO dashboard logs; security vulnerability"
    fix: "Hash all identifiers; add a test that greps log output for key patterns"

  - pitfall: "Logging crashes the operation"
    symptom: "json.dumps() raises on an exotic type; service returns 500"
    fix: "Wrap log emission in try/except; use default=str; fire-and-forget"

  - pitfall: "No timestamp in log lines"
    symptom: "Can't correlate events; can't determine order; can't calculate rates"
    fix: "Always include ts field with ISO 8601 UTC"

  - pitfall: "Inconsistent event names"
    symptom: "Some lines say 'tool_call', others say 'call', others say 'invoke'"
    fix: "Define event name enum in the intent; use constants in code"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# COST & PERFORMANCE
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

typical_costs:
  development_complexity: "Simple (20-50 lines of code, zero dependencies)"
  infrastructure_cost: "$0 (stdout captured by platform; no external service)"
  maintenance_effort: "Minimal (add new dimensions as needed)"

typical_performance:
  latency: "< 1ms per log line (json.dumps + print)"
  throughput: "Thousands of lines per second (stdout is fast)"
  storage: "Platform-dependent (DO: ~7 days retention; ELK: configurable)"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# REFERENCES
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

references:
  documentation:
    - "Twelve-Factor App, Factor XI: Logs — https://12factor.net/logs"
    - "structlog best practices — https://www.structlog.org/en/latest/logging-best-practices.html"

  research:
    - "Stripe canonical log lines (internal pattern, widely cited)"
    - "OpenTelemetry MCP semantic conventions — https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/mcp/"
    - "MCP best practices for observability — https://mcp-best-practice.github.io/mcp-best-practice/best-practice/"

  tools:
    - name: "json (stdlib)"
      purpose: "JSON serialization for structured log lines"
      link: "https://docs.python.org/3/library/json.html"

    - name: "jq"
      purpose: "Command-line JSON processor for querying logs"
      link: "https://jqlang.github.io/jq/"

    - name: "doctl apps logs"
      purpose: "Stream or fetch logs from DigitalOcean App Platform"
      link: "https://docs.digitalocean.com/reference/doctl/reference/apps/logs/"

# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# CHANGELOG
# ------------------------------------------------------------------------------

changelog:
  - version: "1.0"
    date: "2026-02-08"
    change: "Initial recipe creation for structured logging pattern"
    reason: |
      IVD MCP Server needs tool call tracking for private alpha. Pattern is
      reusable across any service with a dispatch layer. Based on research:
      Twelve-Factor App (stdout), Stripe (canonical log lines), structlog
      (best practices), OpenTelemetry MCP conventions (semantic fields).
    author: "Leo Celis"
    evidence: "Research: structlog docs, OpenTelemetry MCP semconv, Twelve-Factor App"
